title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Large Rapamycin Aging Trial Funded by NIA
description: UT Health San Antonio is launching a multi-phase, NIA-funded clinical trial of rapamycin in 84 older adults, focused on precise dosing, safety, and long-term healthspan effects. This represents a major step toward evidence-based guidance for rapamycin's anti-aging use, moving beyond the off-label speculation that has defined the field.

title: uPAR-Targeted CAR T Cells Show Promise Against Solid Tumors and Senescent Cells
description: Memorial Sloan Kettering researchers published preclinical results in Cell showing uPAR-targeted CAR T cells can eliminate both cancer cells and senescent cells in the tumor microenvironment. This dual-targeting approach could bridge oncology and senolytic therapy, offering durable remissions in ovarian cancer models.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144M to Build Infrastructure for First Large-Scale Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program has awarded $144 million to seven teams to lay groundwork for Phase 3 aging trials, including testing rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, and semaglutide in healthy adults in their 60s. This is the first serious U.S. government effort to move geroprotective compounds from animal models into large human trials.

title: Senolytics Pipeline Narrows Focus: 2026 Status Report Shows Translational Progress
description: A comprehensive 2026 pipeline review shows senolytics are evolving from broad anti-aging claims toward biomarker-guided, disease-specific therapies. A Nature Aging study comparing 21 senolytic agents identified ABT263 and ARV825 as top performers, while mitochondrial stress has emerged as a key mechanism for enhancing senolytic effectiveness.

title: Longevity Biotech Q1 2026: $3.74B Raised as Capital Flows Accelerate 56% Over 2025
description: DLT data shows Q1 2026 recorded 49 longevity biotech financing events totaling approximately $3.74 billion -- a 56% increase over Q1 2025. The projected full-year range of $8-9 billion signals that longevity biotech is crossing from niche thesis to mainstream investment category.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Trial
description: NIA-funded study will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and long-term healthspan effects in approximately 84 older adults over six months with six months of follow-up.

title: uPAR-Targeted CAR T Cells Wipe Out Solid Tumors and Senescent Cells in Preclinical Models
description: MSK researchers developed CAR T cells targeting uPAR protein on both cancer and senescent cells, achieving durable remissions in ovarian cancer mouse models.

title: ARPA-H PROSPR Program Awards $144M for Large-Scale Aging Intervention Trials
description: Seven teams will build infrastructure for Phase 3 trials of rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, and semaglutide in healthy older adults to establish pathway to regulatory approval for aging interventions.

title: Fight Aging! Reports on Partial Reprogramming Challenges and First Clinical Trial
description: Life Biosciences has commenced the first human trial of partial epigenetic reprogramming (ER-100) for optic neuropathies, though significant safety challenges remain before broader body application.

title: Academic Rapamycin Trials Launch to Answer Dosing Questions for Anti-Aging Use
description: New academic trials aim to establish evidence-based rapamycin dosing for aging, as over 300 people now use it off-label but generate minimal useful clinical data.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026: $3.74B Raised in Q1 Signals Breakout Year
description: DLT data shows 49 Q1 financing events totaling $3.74B, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with full-year projections of $8-9 billion.

title: Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Narrowing Focus on Biomarker-Driven Approaches
description: Comprehensive review shows senolytics shifting to disease-specific, biomarker-guided therapy. Nature Aging study ranks ABT263 and ARV825 as top performers across 21 agents tested.

title: Genes Influence Human Lifespan Far More Than Thought: Over 50% Heritable
description: Weizmann Institute study in Science shows lifespan heritability exceeds 55% when extrinsic mortality is accounted for, doubling previous estimates of 20-25%.

title: cGAS-STING Signal Reprogramming: A New Strategy for Ovarian and Systemic Aging
description: Researchers propose signal reprogramming to rewire cGAS-STING inflammatory output toward repair rather than destruction, offering a more nuanced approach than blanket pathway inhibition.

title: Longevity Market Funding Tracker: 12 Pure-Play Deals Total $530M+ Through Jan 2026
description: Function Health's $298M Series B at $2.5B valuation led the period, with diverse bets on cellular reprogramming, mTOR inhibitors, and aging biomarkers.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Comprehensive Pros, Cons, and Future Perspectives Review
description: Major review in Frontiers confirms rapamycin as the most replicated life-extending drug in mammals but cautions that current animal data does not yet support it as a reliable human intervention.

title: Low-Dose Rapamycin Reduces p21 Senescence Marker in Human Immune Cells
description: Research published in Aging Cell shows low-dose rapamycin significantly reduced p21, a key marker of cellular senescence, in immune cells from older adults.

title: Life Biosciences Begins First-in-Human Partial Reprogramming Trial for Vision Loss
description: FDA greenlighted Life Biosciences' IND for ER-100, a partial epigenetic reprogramming gene therapy for glaucoma and NAION, marking a historic first for cellular rejuvenation therapy.

title: BCL-2 Inhibition Shows Senolytic Promise in Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis
description: New research in Nature Communications demonstrates BCL-2 inhibition can selectively target senescent fibroblasts in IPF, opening a potential therapeutic avenue.

title: Targeting Longevity Conference in Berlin: Systems Biology Meets Translational Science
description: The International Conference on Targeting Longevity 2026 in Berlin brings together leaders in aging research, systems biology, and longevity investment strategy.

title: Alex Zhavoronkov: Longevity Biotech Crossing from Thesis to Mainstream in 2026
description: Insilico Medicine CEO Alex Zhavoronkov highlights DLT dealflow intelligence now powering BD for a Top 10 pharma, signaling institutional validation of longevity biotech.

title: Longevity Investing 2026: Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Comprehensive analysis values longevity biotech market at $30.7B in 2026, projected to reach $52.8B by 2035, with detailed investment framework across reprogramming, senolytics, and AI drug discovery.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Updated landscape review profiles key players including Altos Labs, Rubedo Life Sciences, Genflow Biosciences, and Rejuvenate Bio across reprogramming, senolytics, and gene therapy.

title: Longevity.Technology Week 14 Roundup: Can Aging Be Treated Like a Disease?
description: Weekly roundup covers regulatory frameworks for aging-as-disease, the latest DLT intelligence platform developments, and key longevity investment signals.

title: Advanced Antiaging Therapies: What to Expect in 2026 and Beyond
description: Expert review ranks mesenchymal stem cell preparations as the most advanced anti-aging candidates in clinical development, with three positive RCTs for skeletal muscle frailty.

title: Longevity Center Europe Reports Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Population Average
description: DunedinPACE testing of 80+ individuals showed average aging pace of 0.78 (22% slower), with at least 10 Polish clients beating Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65 score.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Data-Driven Refinements
description: Latest Blueprint update shifts from rigid rules to adaptive data-driven refinement, expanding biomarker tracking to organ-specific metrics and real-time wearable data.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Mitochondrial Stress Boosts Senolytic Effectiveness
description: Nature Aging paper shows mitochondrial stress is key to senolytic efficacy. Additional highlights include SIRT6 brain protection and NK cell rejuvenation findings.

title: Exercise-Induced Exosomes May Serve as Systemic Anti-Aging Therapeutics
description: New research in Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology explores muscle-derived exerkines as mediators of systemic exercise benefits and potential therapeutic applications.

title: Fecal Microbiota Transplant from Old to Young Mice Accelerates Aging
description: Fight Aging! newsletter highlights study showing FMT from aged mice accelerates aging in younger recipients, underscoring the microbiome's role in the aging process.
